---
title: "Practice: Effective And Honest Scales"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
```

# Goal

Practice choosing scales that make patterns visible without misleading readers.

# Suggested Data

Choose one:

- `data/real/flu_vaccination_age_time_clean.csv`
- `data/real/state_population_ranks.csv`
- `data/simulated/respiratory_booster_coverage.csv`

Helpful module reference: `modules/09_communication-polish/01_effective-and-honest-scales.qmd`.

# Load And Inspect

```{r}
# Load your dataset here.
```

```{r}
# Inspect variables and ranges.
```

# Build A First Plot

```{r}
# Build a first version of your plot here.
```

# Revise The Scale

Try a different axis limit, break spacing, coordinate choice, or percent formatting.

```{r}
# Build a revised scale version here.
```

# Interpret

> TODO: Which scale choice is more honest for the comparison? What tradeoff does it make?
